facts_disclosed: Switched to Logic Pro from Ableton

turns.content: yooo ok so I need to talk about this because everyone in my circle thinks I'm losing my mind. you know I've been an Ableton person for YEARS right?? like that was my whole ting. every beat, every mix, everything went through Ableton!!
Ableton's a solid DAW -- it's a lot of producers' go-to. So what happened? Did you hit a wall with it, or are you trying something new?
ok so hear me out!! I switched to Logic Pro!! I KNOW I KNOW. but listen. I've been on it for like 2 months now and I'm not going back anytime soon
That's a big move! Logic's a great DAW too though. What made you pull the trigger on switching?

first of all the STOCK PLUGINS are absolutely INSANE!! like I was spending so much on third party stuff with Ableton and Logic just comes with everything. Alchemy alone is worth the switch still. the synth presets are unreal!!

You're not wrong about that. Alchemy is a beast of a synthesizer -- it can do wavetable, granular, additive, and FM synthesis all in one plugin. And Logic also comes with a really solid collection of effects -- Space Designer for reverb, the vintage EQ collection, and the new compressor models. The value for the price is hard to beat.

EXACTLY!! and it's so much cheaper too!! like Ableton Suite is what, $750? Logic is $200 one time and you get ALL of that. the economics just make sense when you're independent and every dollar counts ahlie

That's a massive difference. $200 for Logic vs $749 for Ableton Suite -- and Logic gives you free updates for life. For an independent producer watching their budget, that's a smart financial decision. How's the workflow transition been though? That's usually where people struggle.

ok so the first 2 weeks were ROUGH I'm not gonna lie!! I kept reaching for shortcuts that didn't exist. my brain was still in Ableton mode. but once I got past that hump?? the arrangement view in Logic is actually FIRE!! it feels more visual to me. like I can see the whole song laid out proper

That's a common experience with DAW switches -- the first couple weeks feel like learning to write with your other hand. But Logic's arrangement view is really well designed. The track stacking, the ability to create arrangement markers and move whole sections around, and the comping features are all top-notch. Are you missing anything from Ableton though?

honestly?? session view. I miss that for when I'm doing live ting and just jamming ideas. Logic doesn't really have an equivalent. but for actual PRODUCTION? for sitting in the lab and building a track from scratch?? Logic is hitting different right now!!

That's a fair trade-off. Session View is really Ableton's unique strength -- there's nothing quite like it for live performance and improvisation. If you're mainly doing studio production work right now, Logic's a great fit. Just something to keep in mind if you get more into live sets -- you might want to keep Ableton around for that side of things. How are your mixes sounding in Logic so far?

the mixes are sounding CLEAN!! the Flex Pitch is wild for vocal editing too. I did a riddim last week and the low end sits so much better for some reason?? maybe it's the compressors. I don't know what apple puts in their sauce but the riddim just HITS in Logic. I'm sold for now still!!

A lot of producers say that about Logic's summing and built-in processing -- there's a perceived warmth and cohesion to the mix bus. Could also be that you're approaching the mix with fresh ears since you're in a new environment. Either way, if it's working and you're inspired, that's what matters most. Sounds like you made the right call for where you're at right now.
